info.description: This problem set focuses on viral pathogens and antiviral agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: The Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) is an enveloped member of the Retroviridae family. Which of the following best describes the specific genomic structure found within a mature HIV virion?

options.label: Haploid single-stranded DNA
Diploid positive-sense RNA
Segmented double-stranded RNA
Circular partially double-stranded DNA
Non-segmented negative-sense RNA

q2.prompt: Ganciclovir is significantly more effective than acyclovir for the treatment of serious Cytomegalovirus (CMV) infections in immunocompromised patients. What is the primary biochemical reason CMV is susceptible to ganciclovir but typically resistant to acyclovir?

options.label: CMV lacks a viral-encoded DNA polymerase
CMV produces a specific protein kinase (UL97) that phosphorylates ganciclovir
Acyclovir is unable to penetrate the complex CMV envelope
CMV replicates in the cytoplasm where acyclovir remains inactive
Ganciclovir acts as a pyrophosphate analog while acyclovir does not

q3.prompt: A premature infant born in February is administered palivizumab as prophylaxis to prevent severe lower respiratory tract disease and bronchiolitis. Which viral protein is the specific target of this monoclonal antibody?

options.label: Neuraminidase
Hemagglutinin
Fusion (F) protein
Matrix (M1) protein
RNA-dependent RNA polymerase

q4.prompt: Naked capsid viruses, such as Hepatitis A virus (HAV), are typically transmitted via the fecal-oral route. In contrast, enveloped viruses like HIV are not transmitted this way. Which structural property of HAV primarily allows it to survive transit through the gastrointestinal tract?

options.label: Presence of a lipid-rich lipoprotein envelope
Lack of an envelope, providing resistance to detergents and acidic pH
Use of a viral-encoded reverse transcriptase
Possession of a diploid RNA genome
Helical nucleocapsid symmetry that resists bile salts

q5.prompt: Individuals who are homozygous for the CCR5-delta 32 mutation exhibit significant natural resistance to infection by common macrophage-tropic strains of HIV. Which specific process of the viral life cycle is fundamentally blocked by this genetic variation?

options.label: Production of high-titer neutralizing anti-envelope antibodies
Docking and entry mediated by the coreceptor interaction
Intracellular degradation of viral genomic RNA
Translation of the viral Gag-Pol polyprotein precursor
Syncytium formation between infected CD4+ T cells

q6.prompt: Zika virus is an enveloped flavivirus associated with congenital Zika syndrome and microcephaly in neonates. To establish infection in target cells, such as neural progenitor cells, the virus utilizes which specific cellular receptor for entry?

options.label: CD21
ACE2
AXL
P antigen
Nicotinic AChR

q8.prompt: Parvovirus B19 is a small, non-enveloped DNA virus that can cause a transient aplastic crisis (TAC). To establish infection, the virus must gain entry into its primary target cell, the erythroid progenitor cell. Which host cell component serves as the receptor for viral attachment?

options.label: CD4 receptor
Erythrocyte blood group P antigen
ICAM-1 adhesion molecule
ACE2 receptor
Nicotinic acetylcholine receptor

q9.prompt: A clinician is managing an elderly patient with a laboratory-confirmed Influenza A infection. If the clinician prescribes amantadine, which specific step of the viral life cycle is being targeted?

options.label: Viral attachment to host cell receptors
Uncoating of the viral genome within the host cell
Cleavage of sialic acid residues for progeny release
Integration of viral DNA into the host cell chromosome
Proteolytic processing of viral polyproteins

q10.prompt: A patient with HIV who has been poorly adherent to their antiretroviral therapy (ART) is found to have high-level resistance to the Protease Inhibitor (PI) class of drugs. This mechanism of resistance allows the virus to bypass the drug-induced blockade of which essential life cycle step?

options.label: Integration of the linear viral DNA into the host genome
Cleavage of the Gag-Pol polyprotein required for viral maturation
Fusion of the viral envelope mediated by the gp41 protein
Synthesis of a DNA copy from the genomic RNA template
Binding of the gp120 glycoprotein to the CCR5 coreceptor
